name: web-research
description: Use this skill for requests related to web research; it provides a structured approach to conducting comprehensive web research
capabilities: Creates organized research folders and detailed research plans
Delegates research tasks to parallel subagents for efficiency
Synthesizes findings from multiple web sources
Generates well-cited research reports with source URLs
Manages research workflow systematically with file-based communication
author: langchain-ai
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation and JSON metadata with no executable code. Static scanner flagged documentation syntax (backticks, code blocks) and metadata fields as security patterns, but all findings are false positives. The skill provides research methodology instructions only.
valueStatement: Research complex topics efficiently without getting overwhelmed. This skill organizes your research into manageable subtopics and synthesizes findings into comprehensive reports.
limitations: Limited to 3-5 web searches per subtopic for efficiency
Cannot access paywalled or subscription-only content
Requires manual planning to define research scope
Dependent on search engine availability and results quality

useCases.title: Research paper preparation
useCases.description: Gather comprehensive information on complex topics with proper citations for academic writing

useCases.title: Market research reports
useCases.description: Compare competitors, analyze trends, and synthesize market intelligence efficiently

useCases.title: Topic investigation
useCases.description: Research background information for articles, videos, or presentations with verified sources

promptTemplates.title: Basic topic research
promptTemplates.prompt: Research the history and current applications of renewable energy in developing countries

promptTemplates.title: Comparative analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare the top 3 project management tools for remote teams, focusing on features and pricing

promptTemplates.title: Complex investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: Investigate the impact of AI on healthcare diagnostics, including benefits, risks, and regulatory challenges

promptTemplates.title: Market research
promptTemplates.prompt: Research the electric vehicle market in Europe, including major players, growth trends, and government policies

bestPractices: Always create a research plan before starting to stay organized and avoid duplicated effort
Use clear, specific subtopics to prevent overlap between parallel research agents
Review all findings files before creating your final synthesis report
antiPatterns: Skipping the planning phase leads to disorganized research and redundant searches
Having subagents return findings directly instead of saving to files creates data loss risk
Over-researching with more than 5 searches per subtopic wastes resources without adding value

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Claude versions?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with Claude and Claude Code. The skill uses standard tools available in both platforms.

faq.question: How many subtopics should I create?
faq.answer: Use 1-2 for simple queries, 3 for comparisons, and 3-5 for complex investigations. Avoid overlap between subtopics.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my existing research workflow?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill creates standard markdown files you can use with any note-taking or documentation system.

faq.question: Is my research data safe?
faq.answer: All files are saved locally in your working directory. No data is sent to external services beyond web searches.

faq.question: What if subagents cannot find enough information?
faq.answer: Review the findings files to identify gaps. You can spawn additional subagents or adjust your search queries.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual web searching?
faq.answer: This skill provides structure and parallelization. You get organized findings files instead of scattered browser tabs.
